methods.description: Process for growing Phaeodactylum tricornutum strain CCMP2561 in controlled conditions to ensure optimal growth for experiments.

steps.description: The Phaeodactylum tricornutum strain CCMP2561 (NCMA) was grown axenically at 20°C in vented cap flasks containing silica-free F/2 medium (Sigma G0154) with 40% Sea Salts (Sigma S9883).
steps.searchable_summary: grow Phaeodactylum tricornutum in silica-free F/2 medium at 20°C

steps.description: Incubators were equipped with white neon light tubes providing an illumination of approximately 120 µmol photons m −2 s −1 and a photoperiod of 12 h light/12 h dark.
steps.searchable_summary: set incubator light conditions to 120 µmol photons m−2 s−1 with 12h light/12h dark cycle

methods.description: Procedures for selecting transformed cells using Nourseothricin (NAT) and 5-FOA to identify successful genetic modifications.

steps.description: Cells transformed with the NAT selection plasmid were collected two or four days posttransformation and spread on two F/2 agar plates with 300 µg ml −1 NAT.
steps.searchable_summary: spread transformed cells on F/2 agar plates with 300 µg ml−1 NAT

steps.description: After three weeks, colonies were re-streaked on fresh 10-cm 1% agar plates containing 300 µg ml −1 NAT.
steps.searchable_summary: re-streak colonies on fresh agar plates with 300 µg ml−1 NAT

steps.description: NAT-transformants were then transferred onto F/2 agar plates containing 50 µg mL −1 uracil and 100 µg mL −1 5-FOA.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer NAT-transformants to F/2 agar plates with uracil and 5-FOA

methods.description: Design and assembly of TALEN pairs for targeted genetic modifications using TALE-NT and Golden Gate cloning.

steps.description: The TALEN pairs were designed using TALE-NT with two 20-bp recognition sites separated by an 18bp spacer.
steps.searchable_summary: design TALEN pairs using TALE-NT with specified recognition sites

steps.description: All TALEN constructs were assembled through Golden Gate cloning and cloned into TALEN scaffolds.
steps.searchable_summary: assemble TALEN constructs using Golden Gate cloning

methods.description: Design and assembly of CRISPR-Cas9 constructs using gRNAs selected by CRISPOR and cloning into expression vectors.

steps.description: Guide RNAs (gRNAs) were selected using the CRISPOR tool based on the Moreno-Mateos score and the absence of predicable off targets.
steps.searchable_summary: select gRNAs using CRISPOR tool

steps.description: The gRNAs were cloned under control of the U6 promoter in a vector derived from pKSdiaCas9.
steps.searchable_summary: clone gRNAs into U6 promoter vector

methods.description: Transformation of cells using biolistic bombardment with DNA-coated gold particles for genetic modification.

steps.description: Cells were collected from exponentially growing cultures and spread onto 1% agar plates containing F/2 medium with 20 g L −1 sea salt.
steps.searchable_summary: spread cells on agar plates with F/2 medium and sea salt

steps.description: Gold particles were coated with DNA using 1.25 M CaCl2 and 20 mM spermidine.
steps.searchable_summary: coat gold particles with DNA using CaCl2 and spermidine

steps.description: Transformations were carried out using the microparticle bombardment method with a burst pressure of 1550 psi and a vacuum of 25 Hg.
steps.searchable_summary: perform microparticle bombardment for transformation

methods.method_name: In Vitro CRISPR-Cas9 RNP Cleavage Assay
methods.description: Assay to test the cleavage efficiency of CRISPR-Cas9 RNP complexes on target DNA in vitro.

steps.description: DNA fragments containing the target site were amplified, purified, and eluted with RNase-free water.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify and purify target DNA fragments

steps.description: CRISPR-Cas9 RNP complexes were assembled using crRNA::tracrRNA duplexes and HiFi Cas9.
steps.searchable_summary: assemble CRISPR-Cas9 RNP complexes

steps.description: Each RNP complex was combined with the corresponding DNA amplicon and the in vitro cleavage reaction allowed to proceed.
steps.searchable_summary: perform in vitro cleavage reaction with RNP complexes

steps.description: Sequential RNase A and proteinase K treatments were performed before gel separation and visualization.
steps.searchable_summary: treat with RNase A and proteinase K, then perform gel separation

methods.description: Characterization of genetic modifications using PCR and allele-specific techniques to identify successful edits.

steps.description: Cell lysates served as the PCR templates.
steps.searchable_summary: use cell lysates as PCR templates

steps.description: Primers listed in Supplementary Table 5 were used to amplify the various loci of interest.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify loci of interest using specific primers

steps.description: The Q5 High Fidelity DNA polymerase was used for general screening purposes.
steps.searchable_summary: use Q5 High Fidelity DNA polymerase for screening

steps.description: The highly selective HiDi DNA polymerase was used for allele-specific PCR, together with primers ending with single nucleotide polymorphisms.
steps.searchable_summary: perform allele-specific PCR with HiDi DNA polymerase

methods.description: Use of NGS technology to quantify the frequency of mutagenic events in PCR products.

steps.description: Locus-specific PCR products were sequenced using NGS technology (S5 Thermofisher).
steps.searchable_summary: sequence PCR products using NGS technology

steps.description: The sequencing and the processing of NGS data was done by the GeT-Biopuces platform using the Ion Torrent Suite software analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: process NGS data using Ion Torrent Suite

steps.description: The percentage of mutagenic event was calculated at position 145 and at +/− 5 bases surrounding this position.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate percentage of mutagenic events at specific positions
